commands.review_summary:
The PR extracts QA-lab parity stable hashing into a shared helper, guards unreadable and cyclic schema values, and adds harness parity regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +45, Tests +110. Total +155 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main reads schema fields inside stableHash without guarding getters or cycles, so a hostile captured schema can throw during parity classification. I did not execute tests because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed; this maintainer-labeled draft PR has no actionable patch defect from review and should stay with normal maintainer merge/check handling.

target.title: fix(qa-lab): guard parity stable hashes
target.body:
## Summary

- Extracts the QA-lab parity stable hash into one shared helper used by both harness parity and runtime parity.
- Guards stable hashing against unreadable object fields, unreadable array entries, and cycles so one bad captured tool schema cannot crash parity classification.
- Adds harness parity regressions for unreadable tool schema objects, collision-prone normalized values, unreadable array entries, and cyclic array schema fields.

## Verification

- Red proof before the fix: an inline `tsx` probe against `buildHarnessParityCell()` failed with `schema getter exploded`.
- `./node_modules/.bin/tsx --eval '<collision probe>'` -> `tool-schema`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/qa-lab/src/harness-parity.test.ts` -> 1 file / 7 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/qa-lab/src/stable-hash.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/harness-parity.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/runtime-parity.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/harness-parity.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint extensions/qa-lab/src/stable-hash.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/harness-parity.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/runtime-parity.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/harness-parity.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` -> clean, overall 0.84
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` found two real findings; both were fixed.
- Final `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` -> clean, overall 0.86

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: QA-lab parity hashing could throw while digesting a captured tool schema with hostile getters or cycles, aborting parity classification instead of reporting a tool-schema drift.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw linked worktree on Node/Vitest using the repo extension QA test config.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/qa-lab/src/harness-parity.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: harness parity test passed 1 file / 7 tests, including regressions for unreadable object fields, unreadable array entries, and cyclic array schema fields.

Observed result after fix: unreadable captured schema data no longer crashes stable hashing; parity returns `tool-schema` drift for unreadable-vs-readable schema shapes.

What was not tested: broad `pnpm check:changed` did not run. `blacksmith testbox list --all` is unauthenticated, AWS Crabbox returned coordinator HTTP 401 for run/lease creation, and delegated Blacksmith-through-Crabbox failed on missing Blacksmith auth.
target.author: vincentkoc
